---
title: "LRPAL05: Aligned-40-week per-protocol gain for phonetic spelling (P), off-floor"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
cohort_marginal = pd.read_csv(_here / "cohort_marginal.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPAL05 is the **aligned-40-week per-protocol** single-gain model for phonetic
spelling (`spphon`). Both arms are aligned by intervention onset across ~two
periods (~the 40-week RLI program): the immediate arm onsets at t1 (aligned window
**t1 → t3**) and the wait-list arm at t2, post-crossover (aligned window
**t2 → t4**). One row per child, so there is no child random intercept.

::: {.callout-important title="Off-floor likelihood + per-protocol (NOT randomised)"}

Phonetic spelling is **heavily floored**, so this uses the suite floor rule:
`likelihood="bernoulli_offfloor"` — a **Bernoulli on the off-floor indicator**
(aligned post $> 0$), with no `kappa`; the linear predictor is the log-odds of
coming off the floor and the cohort marginal collapses to an **off-floor risk
difference**.

The design is also **per-protocol, not randomised**: the cohort term
(`beta_cohort`) is confounded by age-at-onset and cohort/timing, so **no**
coefficient is a clean treatment effect — every term is an association.

:::

## Model

$$
(P_{\text{post(aligned)}} > 0) \sim \operatorname{Bernoulli}(\operatorname{expit}(\eta)),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \beta_{\text{cohort}}\,\text{immediate}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(P_{\text{onset}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{onset,std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}).
$$

One row per child; no `kappa` under the off-floor Bernoulli. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (cohort)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary (off-floor log-odds, all associations)

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

All coefficients are off-floor log-odds and every `role` is **association** (the
per-protocol design identifies no causal term). `mean`/`lo`/`hi` = posterior mean +
equal-tailed 95% CrI; `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$.

### Cohort off-floor risk difference (per-protocol, NOT randomised)

```{python}
# | echo: false
cohort_marginal
```

The `trt_*` rows are the **off-floor risk difference** between arms at the aligned
endpoints (the items scale collapses to the probability scale; `n_trials = 1`) — a
per-protocol association confounded by age-at-onset and timing, not the randomised
treatment effect.

**Reading these tables.** Per the project's reporting conventions (`METHODS.md`,
"Interpret"), lead with the **interval**, not the point estimate, and read
`prob_positive` / `prob_trt_pos` as evidence of **direction, not magnitude**. At this
design's small per-arm sample (~26–28) a point estimate is on average
magnitude-inflated (a Type-M / winner's-curse effect). A ROPE-anchored **magnitude**
read is **deliberately deferred** for this per-protocol family — and for P the
off-floor risk-difference δ is in any case only a placeholder pending the education
lead; see `notes/202606261343-lrpal-aligned-design.md`.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
